key_departures.impact: Trusted backup who went 6 TDs / 0 INTs with a 79% completion rate in relief duty. Leaves Green Bay without a proven backup QB. Miami's Jeff Hafley immediately targeted him as a starter. Will likely generate a 3rd-round compensatory pick in 2027.

Led the Packers in receiving in 2025 with 55 catches, 724 yards, and 6 TDs. Was Jordan Love's most trusted target. Departure creates a need for a WR2/slot contributor, though Green Bay retains Jayden Reed, Christian Watson, Matthew Golden, and Dontayvion Wicks. Expected to generate a 4th-round compensatory pick in 2027.

Four straight 100-tackle seasons, 128 tackles in 2025. Packers declined his 5th-year option and proactively traded for Zaire Franklin before Walker hit the market. Departure is mitigated by the Franklin acquisition. Expected to generate a 5th/6th-round compensatory pick in 2027.

Rotational EDGE who outperformed Rashan Gary for stretches of the season and totaled 11.5 sacks in four years with Green Bay. Departure further depletes a thin EDGE group. Micah Parsons, Lukas Van Ness, and Barryn Sorrell remain.

Three-year starter at LT, protecting Jordan Love's blind side. 93.8% pass block win rate (11th among qualifying tackles in 2025). The Packers deliberately did not pursue re-signing him, instead developing 2024 first-round pick Jordan Morgan to take over at LT. Expected to generate a 3rd-round compensatory pick in 2027.

Traded to Dallas for a 2027 4th-rounder. Gary had 7.5 sacks in 2025 but zero sacks or TFLs in the final nine games. Green Bay saved ~$11M in cap space. Combined with Enagbare's departure, EDGE is now a significant need.

Released after suffering a season-ending ankle injury in 2025. Re-signing of Sean Rhyan at C and restructuring of Aaron Banks' deal at LG sealed Jenkins' fate. Packers saved $19.53M in cap space. His IOL depth is replaced internally.

Cut just one year after signing a 4yr/$48M deal. Hobbs battled injuries and struggled at outside corner. Released as a post-June 1 cut, saving $8.838M when it processes. Thins an already-thin CB room.

Traded to Indy in exchange for LB Zaire Franklin. Wooden was Green Bay's best interior run stopper in 2025, playing 52.4% of defensive snaps. His departure is the primary driver of IDL becoming the team's most pressing need.

signings.trade_details: GB sent DT Colby Wooden to Colts

contract.notes: 2 years remaining on existing 3yr/$31.26M extension; no guaranteed money remaining. 2026 cap hit ~$7M. Can be released at $0 cap charge if cut before roster cutdown.

signings.analysis: Franklin was the NFL's leading tackler and a Pro Bowl/All-Pro selection in 2024, recording 173 tackles. He struggled significantly in 2025 (PFF's 87th of 88 qualifying LBs, worst coverage grade among starters). The Packers acquired him knowing Quay Walker would walk, giving them a veteran presence alongside Edgerrin Cooper. The contract structure — no guarantees, effectively a 1-year prove-it situation for cap purposes — signals a bridge rather than a long-term solution. LB need is partially addressed; the draft should still be on the table for a coverage LB.

contract.notes: $11M signing bonus confirmed; multi-year real commitment at center.

signings.analysis: Rhyan moved to center late in 2025 after Elgton Jenkins' season-ending ankle injury and excelled, logging 400+ snaps at a position he'd barely played before. The Packers acted quickly, re-signing him to a 3yr/$33M deal with an $11M signing bonus before the market opened. Combined with Aaron Banks (restructured) at LG and Jordan Morgan at RG, the interior OL need is fully resolved. The contract is a real commitment — not a bridge. IOL drops off the board as a draft priority.

contract.notes: 2yr/$10M base with upside to $10.5M. Below starter-level AAV for CB market ($12M+). This is a depth/competition signing, not a starter-level investment.

signings.analysis: St-Juste is a 6-foot-3 press corner who revitalized his career in LA in 2025, allowing a 68.3 passer rating in coverage. He started just 2 games, primarily a backup role. The Packers signed him for below starter money and designated him explicitly as a depth/competition piece alongside Keisean Nixon and Carrington Valentine. The release of Nate Hobbs the same day made this even more of a horizontal move than a net upgrade. CB remains a significant need — this signing adds competition but does not solve the CB2 problem at starter quality.

contract.notes: Signed before restricted free agency tender deadline to lock him up early.

signings.analysis: Brooks is a pass-blocking specialist and special teams contributor. His re-signing secures RB depth behind Josh Jacobs. At $2.4M AAV this is well below any market threshold for meaningful impact. RB was not a need, and this changes nothing — purely roster maintenance.

contract.notes: One-year deal at approximately $2.5M per reporting. Core special teams ace.

signings.analysis: Niemann is a core special teams ace and LB depth body. Re-signing him is roster maintenance. Does not meaningfully address any team need.

contract.notes: Up to $3.75M on a one-year deal per Pat McAfee Show reporting.

signings.analysis: Kinnard served as Green Bay's swing tackle and jumbo package lineman. His re-signing provides OL depth insurance. With Rhyan locked in at C and the IOL otherwise set, this is pure depth. No meaningful needs impact.

contract.notes: Player self-reported return via Instagram. Contract terms not yet disclosed.

signings.analysis: Backup LB and special teams contributor. Re-signing adds LB depth behind Franklin and Cooper. No meaningful needs impact — depth maintenance only.

contract.notes: One-year deal per Jordan Schultz/ESPN confirmed. Exact financials not yet disclosed. If APY >= $3M, offsets a 2027 5th-round compensatory pick.

signings.analysis: Pure special teams/return specialist addition. Moore averaged 27.5 yards per kick return and 11.6 yards per punt return with the 49ers in 2025 — nearly double Romeo Doubs' 6.3 punt return average. Addresses Green Bay's dead-last ranking in punt return average (5.6 yds). Does NOT replace Doubs as a receiving option — Moore had just 5 catches for 87 yards in 2025. WR need for a pass-catching receiver remains unchanged; this only fills the return game void.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Colby Wooden traded to Colts (was starting NT, played 52.4% of defensive snaps). Rashan Gary trade also removed an EDGE/hybrid. No IDL signed in free agency. Devonte Wyatt is the only established IDL remaining. DT/NT becomes the single most pressing draft need for Green Bay heading into April.

Both Rashan Gary (traded to Cowboys) and Kingsley Enagbare (signed Jets) are gone. Remaining depth is Micah Parsons + Lukas Van Ness + Barryn Sorrell. Parsons is elite but losing two rotational pieces creates a severe depth problem. EDGE is now tied with IDL as the most critical defensive need.

Nate Hobbs was cut (post-June 1 designation), eliminating one body. Benjamin St-Juste signed (2yr/$10M) but at below-starter AAV — depth/competition, not a starter solution. Nixon and Valentine remain but were inconsistent starters. CB need is unchanged — still a tier 2 priority for the draft, potentially round 1 or 2.

Rasheed Walker departed as a UFA (unsigned as of March 11). Jordan Morgan (2024 first-round pick) is the presumptive starter at LT, but this is an unproven quantity — Morgan has not started an NFL game. No OT was signed in free agency. OT escalates to a meaningful draft need; a developmental OT or veteran swing tackle could still be added.

Malik Willis departed to Miami on a $67.5M deal. Green Bay is now without a proven backup behind Jordan Love. Willis was trusted enough to be used as an emergency starter (6 TDs, 0 INTs in relief). The backup QB spot is now an empty need — likely addressable in mid-rounds of the draft or late free agency.

Romeo Doubs departed (4yr/~$70M, Patriots). However, Green Bay retains Jayden Reed, Christian Watson, Matthew Golden, and Dontayvion Wicks. Skyy Moore adds as a returner/depth body only. The WR room has young talent but loses its most experienced, reliable option. WR is a moderate need — ideally addressed in rounds 2-4 for a possession/slot complement, not round 1.

Zaire Franklin trade (for Colby Wooden) directly replaces Quay Walker as the projected starter alongside Edgerrin Cooper. Franklin had 173 tackles and All-Pro honors in 2024 but regressed sharply in 2025. The contract has no guaranteed money — he's a 1-year bridge for practical purposes. LB drops from a critical need to a moderate one; team would still benefit from a coverage LB in rounds 3-5.

Sean Rhyan re-signed to 3yr/$33M ($11M signing bonus) at center. Aaron Banks restructured at LG. Jordan Morgan expected at RG/swing. Elgton Jenkins released but his role is covered. IOL is effectively resolved — not a draft priority.

key_additions.contract_summary: TRADE (Colby Wooden to Colts) — 2yrs remaining, $7M cap hit
key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl tackler (173 tackles in 2024) acquired to replace Quay Walker at MLB alongside Edgerrin Cooper
Packers lock up emerging center to 3-year deal after his breakout stretch run at a new position, solving IOL need
6-foot-3 press corner adds size and competition to a thin CB room, though not a starter-level investment
Elite return specialist (27.5 yd KR avg, 11.6 yd PR avg) signs to overhaul Green Bay's NFL-worst punt return unit
Pass-blocking RB and ST contributor retained to provide depth behind Josh Jacobs

key_losses.headline: Team's leading receiver (55 rec, 724 yds, 6 TDs in 2025) departs, leaving a void as Jordan Love's most trusted target
Trusted backup QB (6 TDs, 0 INTs in relief duty) leaves to start in Miami, leaving Green Bay without a proven No. 2
Four-time 100-tackle linebacker departs, mitigated by Zaire Franklin trade but coverage LB void remains

key_losses.contract_summary: TRADE — Cowboys sent 2027 4th-round pick to GB
key_losses.headline: $96M pass rusher traded as a cap casualty after zero sacks in final 9 games; GB saves ~$11M and adds draft capital
Reliable rotational EDGE (11.5 sacks in 4 GB seasons) departs alongside Gary, gutting depth off the edge
3-year LT starter walks in free agency; Jordan Morgan (2024 1st-round pick) inherits blind side with no NFL starts

key_losses.contract_summary: TRADED to Colts for Zaire Franklin
key_losses.headline: Starting nose tackle and best interior run stopper traded, leaving IDL as Green Bay's most urgent draft need
$48M free-agent bust released after one injury-plagued season; adds $8.84M cap relief in June
Multi-time Pro Bowler released after season-ending ankle injury; Rhyan extension and Banks restructure made him redundant

notes: Rasheed Walker had not yet signed with a new team as of March 11 (league year opening day). His departure from GB is certain — he was not tendered or re-signed.
Skyy Moore's contract financials not yet publicly disclosed. One-year deal confirmed by ESPN and Jordan Schultz. APY is material due to compensatory pick offset implications.
Kristian Welch contract terms undisclosed — player self-reported return via social media; confirmed per multiple beat reporters.
Nate Hobbs cap savings ($8.84M) will not register until June 1, 2026 post-June 1 designation.

Green Bay does not hold a 2026 first-round pick (traded to Dallas in the Micah Parsons deal). Draft capital is therefore at a premium, amplifying the importance of the IDL and EDGE needs entering the draft.
OverTheCap listed GB cap space at ~$14.38M as of March 11; lombardiave.com estimated $31.44M pre-St-Juste. OTC figure used as post-FA baseline as it accounts for executed transactions.
Benjamin St-Juste guarantee structure TBD — contract not yet officially processed as of updated_through date.
